title: Natural insect deterrent spray
subtitle: A witch hazel and essential oil spray to make outdoor and indoor spaces less attractive to insects
excerpt: This spray uses witch hazel as a carrier for citronella, peppermint, lavender, and eucalyptus essential oils. It does not kill insects; it makes an area less appealing to them through scent. Spray onto surfaces, fabrics, and around doorways and windows. Reapply every 2 to 3 hours outdoors. Not a pesticide.
sourceNotes: Natural insect deterrent spray. Witch hazel as solvent-carrier for essential oils in water. Citronella (Cymbopogon nardus) essential oil: recognised insect deterrent; used at up to 5% in room/environment products. Peppermint, eucalyptus, lavender: supporting deterrents. Total essential oil load 3 to 5% for environmental spray (not on skin). Distilled water dilutes to reduce potency where full-strength is unnecessary. Not registered as a pesticide; makes environment less attractive to insects rather than killing them.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally for a larger batch. Keep in a spray bottle and shake before each use.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 6 months in a sealed spray bottle. Shake before each use. Discard if the witch hazel evaporates significantly and the remaining liquid becomes too concentrated.

glossaryTerms.definition: An essential oil distilled from Cymbopogon nardus (citronella grass). Its sharp lemony scent masks the carbon dioxide and lactic acid signals that attract insects. Most effective against flying insects when used in enclosed or semi-enclosed spaces.

A product that makes an environment less attractive to insects through scent, rather than killing them. It must be reapplied regularly as the essential oils evaporate. Not the same as a pesticide or insecticide.

for surfaces, curtains, and door frames, not a skin product. For use around children under 2, omit eucalyptus essential oil. Witch hazel disperses the essential oils; the mixture will still separate on standing, so shake before every use. Isopropyl alcohol is flammable; keep the spray away from open flames.

items.prepNote: Supporting deterrent; also scent-balances the blend

content.text: Combine witch hazel and water in the spray bottle. Add all essential oils. Cap and shake for 30 seconds to disperse the oils. Label with the contents and date.
Shake before every use. Spray around doorframes, window sills, and curtains. Reapply every 2 to 3 hours for ongoing deterrence, or after rain outdoors. Do not spray directly onto food surfaces.
6 months in a sealed spray bottle. Keep away from direct sunlight.
